Variables.Name: bLowFreq_Enabled
Variables.Description: Is low frequency noise enabled.

Variables.Name: Frequency
Variables.Description: The frequency of noise points.

Variables.Name: Frequency_LowRange
Variables.Description:
If not 0, then the frequency will select a random value in the range
	 		[Frequency_LowRange..Frequency]

Variables.Name: NoiseRange
Variables.Description: The noise point ranges.

Variables.Name: NoiseRangeScale
Variables.Description: A scale factor that will be applied to the noise range.

Variables.Name: bNRScaleEmitterTime
Variables.Description:
If true,  the NoiseRangeScale will be grabbed based on the emitter time.
	 	If false, the NoiseRangeScale will be grabbed based on the particle time.

Variables.Name: NoiseSpeed
Variables.Description: The speed with which to move each noise point.

Variables.Name: bSmooth
Variables.Description: Whether the noise movement should be smooth or 'jerky'.

Variables.Name: NoiseLockRadius
Variables.Description: Default target-point information to use if the beam method is endpoint.

Variables.Name: bNoiseLock
Variables.Description: INTERNAL - Whether the noise points should be locked.

Variables.Name: bOscillate
Variables.Description: Whether the noise points should be oscillate.

Variables.Name: NoiseLockTime
Variables.Description: How long the  noise points should be locked - 0.0 indicates forever.

Variables.Name: NoiseTension
Variables.Description: The tension to apply to the tessellated noise line.

Variables.Name: bUseNoiseTangents
Variables.Description: If true, calculate tangents at each noise point.

Variables.Name: NoiseTangentStrength
Variables.Description: The strength of noise tangents, if enabled.

Variables.Name: NoiseTessellation
Variables.Description: The amount of tessellation between noise points.

Variables.Name: bTargetNoise
Variables.Description:
Whether to apply noise to the target point (or end of line in distance mode...)
	 	If true, the beam could potentially 'leave' the target...

Variables.Name: FrequencyDistance
Variables.Description:
The distance at which to deposit noise points.
	 	If 0.0, then use the static frequency value.
	 	If not, distribute noise points at the given distance, up to the static Frequency value.
	 	At that point, evenly distribute them along the beam.

Variables.Name: bApplyNoiseScale
Variables.Description: If true, apply the noise scale to the beam.

Variables.Name: NoiseScale
Variables.Description:
The scale factor to apply to noise range.
	 	The lookup value is determined by dividing the number of noise points present by the 
	 	maximum number of noise points (Frequency).
